(and others), but we are actually trying to `sorry` the entire statement that two such maps are equal.

This is very confusing. 

But perhaps we can argue as follows:
The two linear maps have the same `toFun`, because for every `y`, `(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha (m • x) y).integral f = m* (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f`, and `m* • (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f` is interpreted as `(m* : ℂ) • ((cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f : ℂ) = m* * ((cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f)`, which is equal to `(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha (m • x) y).integral f`.

But the RHS is `(starRingEnd ℂ) m • { ... }`, which is `m* • { ... }`, so its `toFun y` is indeed `m* * ( (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f )`.

Hence, `(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha (m • x) y).integral f = m* * ( (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f )`, so their `toFun` parts are equal.

Thus, if we can prove that `toFun` parts are equal, then Lean will also need to verify the `map_add'` and `map_smul'` fields (which are currently `sorry`ed), but perhaps those will be automatically satisfied if the `toFun` is correct. 

But in reality, the `sorry` is in the last line, which is the entire equality of the linear maps. 

But perhaps the rest of the fields are not required because Lean's equality of structures will try to prove them automatically?

Alternatively, perhaps this Lean code is not correctly matching the intended theorem statement. 

But in any case, the core is that for each `y`, we have `(cfc_toComplexMeasure ha (m • x) y).integral f = m* (cfc_toComplexMeasure ha x y).integral f`.
